ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Ka a Yesu mɩ bɔ̃ɔn a fɔɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a kpɛ̃ɔ na yi a wʊ sɛ̃. Ka a wʊ lɩɛb a nɩyɔɔ bala pɔ sʊʊr, “An lɛ sɩɩr a n fuu?”
translated: When Jesus was in the house, his disciples came to him. When they had gone out, the crowd came to him and said, "What do you want me to do for you?"

ref: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
text: Be lɛ a poturbo pãa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro 'yɛr lɛ wʊ 'yɛrɛ.
translated: He said that the son of John Konsuoro said, "Say that I say."

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: A poturbo pie nɩ bʊ̃'yen chen a Galilee a tɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ju a Yesu na wiil ba ɩka ba do.
translated: A storm came upon the disciples from Galilee of Jesus and they went to the house of a certain man.

ref: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: A pɔɔ nãa na yin be a par a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar na wʊ wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ 'yɛr, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ yi be a tẽtɛɛr wa chɛl a Nãa Solomon yɛ̃ 'yɛr, tɩchɛ yɩ kaa a 'lɔ na gar a Solomon ben ka.
translated: The king had a son who was a warrior, and his name was Solomon, the one who would build a temple like the temple of King Solomon.

ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Tɩchɛ ka Yesu 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Vɛ̃ ka a ɩ a lɛ a pʊ̃pãanyã, a vɩɛl na ɩka sɩ ɩ a lɛ jaa Nãaŋmɩn na bʊɔrɔ.” Ka Jɔɔn sɔɔ lɛ a Yesu na 'yɛr.
translated: Jesus said, "He who believes in me will do greater works than these, and whatever you ask in my name, the Father will do it for you." And John wrote the words of Jesus.

ref: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
text: Ala ɩ yɩn sɔɔn de, a anyãna 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Elaija nɩ naa lɩɛb wa.
translated: The son of God said, "The one who is Elijah is not here."

ref: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
text: na ba bʊɔrɔ vuo ban na tu nyɔɔ a Yesu na ba kʊ.
translated: They are speaking words with the name of Jesus that they heard.

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ babãma dẽme, a yɩ sɔɔfʊ na ba jã bon ɛ, n be a yɩ sɛ̃ naa tɩ tãn dabor? N tara nɩ yɩ kãnyir jã ŋmɩnɛ?” Tɩ 'yɛr a daba, “Tɔɔ a bie wa kʊ ma.”
translated: Jesus said, "If someone from your household comes to you and you do not give him a place to rest, will he not be angry with you? Or will you not be held accountable?" He answered and said, "It is not so, for I came not to destroy but to save."

ref: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
text: Yesu den sʊkpar 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩ a nɩyɔɔ a yelsɩ anyã, wʊ maa 'yɛr 'yɛr kʊ ba ka waa lɔb sʊkpa ɛ.
translated: Jesus said, "The word that I have spoken to my people, you must indeed go and walk the path."

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Tɩ lɛ bɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ nɩtɔ̃ lɛ wʊ kaara. Daba ma yaar a wʊ bʊ̃bʊrɔ bar.
translated: He said to them, "The Lord has completed his work, look at the sky. And the earth, the people who live here."

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: kul tɩ ũu a wʊ mãɛ yaa paalaa pɔ wʊn chɩ a kʊsɩr pɔ. Wʊ bil kʊsɩr wa pɔɔ a yaa nɛɛ tɩ na wʊ kul.
translated: They prepare the food for the ceremony, as their ancestors have prepared it. They speak the ancestors' words so that the food is eaten.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Yesu lɛ lɔb a sʊkpa ãsʊɔ kʊ ba, “Nɩtɔ̃ lɛ ɩn de a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ man. Nɩrɛ ãsʊɔ lɛ bʊr bʊ̃bivɩɛl a wʊ pʊɔ pɔ,
translated: Jesus told them a parable, saying, "The kingdom of heaven is like a merchant seeking fine pearls. When he found one pearl of great value, he went and sold all that he had and bought it."

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Bɩɩ ŋmɩnɛ fʊn ɩ 'yɛr kʊ a fʊ yɛbɛ vɛ̃ ka ir a dabile na be a fʊ nɩbir pɔ bar tɩ ba chɛlɛ a dakpɛ̃ɛ na be a fʊ nɩbir pɔ ɛ?
translated: Billy, are you coming to your house with the people from the community and the village?

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Sʊkpar lɛ wʊ lɔɔrɔ wile nɩ ba yelyɔɔ, sʊkpa ãsʊɔ nɩ nyã:
translated: Sukpar is the one who works with them, Sukpa is the one they have.

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: a ta sɛɛn ɩka fʊ ta de a n sɛlbir tɩ yɔ̃ɔ a libie yir pɔ, ka maa ta wa lɩɛb wa ka n nyɛ tʊ̃nɔ̃ dɔɔl ɛ?
translated: He/She is going to see with his/her eyes towards the place and will walk on foot with a satisfied heart. Why are you going to a place without knowing the path?

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: Wʊ vɛ̃n ka a nɩyɔɔ banyãna jɛ̃ tẽe. Wʊ den a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu nɩ a jãmɛ ayi ala dʊl kaa saaju tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan tɩ na wʊ ŋma de kʊ a poturbo ɩka ba de põ a nɩbɛ. Ka a poturbo de põ a nɩbɛ.
translated: We go to the people who are arriving here. We are the ones coming from the village where we live, the road leading to Naaŋmin's village, and our meeting place to discuss.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: N ba tara sɔr na de a n libie ɩn a lɛ ɩn bʊɔrɔ bɩɩ? Bɩɩ fʊ tara nyuur nɩ a n maal vɩla?’
translated: Are you the one who saw my child with me? Is it that the mother of the child is not your wife?

ref: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
text: tɩ sʊʊr, “Nɩ-iraa, ala ɩ fʊ bɔ̃ɔn yele jaa wiil sɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ŋme fʊ?”
translated: Tɩ Sʊʊr said, "Nɩ-iraa, where is your child going to find it?"

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: A jãanʊɔr 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa mʊr, a nɩbɛ waan na baalsɩ nɩ a bala a sɩdɛbɛ na tara a Yesu sɛ̃.
translated: He who addresses the Lord, the Father, our God, and declared the word of power that he bestowed through Jesus Christ.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
text: Ka a nɛɛ wiwiilbe bamɩne pãa jɛ̃ bʊlɔ 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ pʊʊr pɔ, “A daba nyã fere na sɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ Nãaŋmɩn yuor.”
translated: Conjunction
